description: GTFS source catalog for the gtfs feeder. Each entry describes one transit feed: GTFS-Realtime URL(s), optional GTFS Schedule URL(s), Mobility Database source ID, agency tag, route selector, and optional headers. Choose which entries run with the GTFS_SOURCES environment variable: a comma-separated list of names, or '*' for all; when unset, every entry whose 'enabled' flag is true is polled. This packaged catalog intentionally ships only disabled examples/templates because GTFS has no universal default feed. Point GTFS_SOURCES_FILE at a copy of this file to maintain your own catalog. Secrets may be written as ${ENV_VAR} and are expanded from the environment at load time, so keys never live in this file. The legacy GTFS_RT_URLS / GTFS_URLS / MDB_SOURCE_ID / AGENCY / ROUTE / GTFS_RT_HEADERS / GTFS_HEADERS inline configuration still works and takes precedence over this catalog when any legacy feed URL or MDB source ID is set.

sources.name: mbta-boston
sources.description: Massachusetts Bay Transportation Authority (Boston, US) public keyless GTFS-Realtime feeds and public GTFS Schedule archive.

sources.name: mta-nyc
sources.description: Metropolitan Transportation Authority (New York City, US) public keyless GTFS-Realtime feeds covering NYC Transit subway (all divisions: A/C/E, B/D/F/M, G, J/Z, L, N/Q/R/W, 1-7/S, Staten Island Railway), Long Island Rail Road, Metro-North Railroad, and system-wide service alerts, plus the public NYCT subway and borough-bus GTFS Schedule archives. No API key required since the 2023 MTA open-data migration to api-endpoint.mta.info. High volume: the combined feeds emit tens of thousands of trip-update, vehicle-position, and alert entities per poll. Add 'mta-nyc' to GTFS_SOURCES to enable.

sources.name: tfnsw-sydney
sources.description: Transport for NSW (Sydney and New South Wales, Australia) API-key-protected GTFS-Realtime feeds for trains, buses, ferries, light rail, and service alerts. Add 'tfnsw-sydney' to GTFS_SOURCES to enable.

sources.name: keyed-operator-template
sources.description: TEMPLATE - copy this entry for an operator that requires an API key. Replace the URLs and agency tag with the operator's documented GTFS-Realtime and GTFS Schedule endpoints, export SOME_GTFS_KEY in the runtime environment, and set enabled to true.
